title: चण्डी पाठ नवार्ण विधि
devanagariText:
॥ श्री चण्डी पाठ नवार्ण विधि ॥

ॐ ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं चामुण्डायै विच्चे॥

॥ नवार्ण मन्त्र विधि ॥

अस्य श्रीनवार्णमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः।
गायत्री छन्दः। चामुण्डा देवता।
ऐं बीजम्। ह्रीं शक्तिः। क्लीं कीलकम्।
श्रीचामुण्डाप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
खड्गं चक्रगदेषुचापपरिघान्शूलं भुशुण्डीं शिरः
शङ्खं सन्दधतीं करैस्त्रिनयनां सर्वाङ्गभूषावृताम्।
नीलाश्मद्युतिमास्यपाददशकां सेवे महाकालिकां
यामस्तौत्स्वपिते हरौ कमलजो हन्तुं मधुं कैटभम्॥ १॥

अक्षस्रक्परशूगदेषुकुलिशं पद्मं धनुष्कुण्डिकां
दण्डं शक्तिमसिं च चर्म जलजं घण्टां सुराभाजनम्।
शूलं पाशसुदर्शने च दधतीं हस्तैः प्रसन्नाननां
सेवे सैरिभमर्दिनीमिह महालक्ष्मीं सरोजस्थिताम्॥ २॥

घण्टाशूलहलानि शङ्खमुसले चक्रं धनुः सायकं
हस्ताब्जैर्दधतीं घनान्तविलसत्शीतांशुतुल्यप्रभाम्।
गौरीदेहसमुद्भवां त्रिजगतामाधारभूतां महा-
पूर्वामत्र सरस्वतीमनुभजे शुम्भादिदैत्यार्दिनीम्॥ ३॥

॥ नवार्ण जपविधि ॥

ॐ ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं चामुण्डायै विच्चे।
(१०८ बार जप करें)

प्रथमं शैलपुत्री च द्वितीयं ब्रह्मचारिणी।
तृतीयं चन्द्रघण्टेति कूष्माण्डेति चतुर्थकम्॥

पञ्चमं स्कन्दमातेति षष्ठं कात्यायनीति च।
सप्तमं कालरात्रिश्च महागौरीति चाष्टमम्।
नवमं सिद्धिदात्री च नवदुर्गाः प्रकीर्तिताः॥ ४॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
नवार्णं मन्त्रराजानं नवदुर्गासमन्वितम्।
यः पठेच्छ्रद्धया नित्यं सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात्॥ ५॥

॥ इति श्री चण्डी पाठ नवार्ण विधि सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Chandi Path Navarna Vidhi ||

Om Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche ||

|| Navarna Mantra Vidhi ||

Asya Shri Navarna Mantrasya Brahma Rishih |
Gayatri Chhandah | Chamunda Devata |
Aim Bijam | Hrim Shaktih | Klim Kilakam |
Shri Chamunda Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Khadgam Chakra Gadeshuchapa Parighan Shulam Bhushundim Shirah
Shankham Sandadhatim Karais Trinayanam Sarvanga Bhushavritam |
Nilashma Dyutim Asya Pada Dashakam Seve Mahakalikam
Yam Astaut Svapite Harau Kamalajo Hantum Madhum Kaitabham || 1 ||

Akshasrak Parashu Gadeshuku Lisham Padmam Dhanush Kundikam
Dandam Shaktim Asim Cha Charma Jalajam Ghantam Surabhaajanam |
Shulam Pasha Sudarshane Cha Dadhatim Hasthaih Prasannanaanam
Seve Sairibha Mardinim Iha Mahalakshmim Sarojasthitam || 2 ||

Ghanta Shula Halani Shankha Musale Chakram Dhanuh Sayakam
Hastabjar Dadhatim Ghanantavilasat Shitamshu Tulya Prabham |
Gauri Deha Samudbhavam Trijagatam Aadhaarabhutam Maha
Purvam Atra Sarasvatim Anubhaje Shumbhadi Daityaardinim || 3 ||

|| Navarna Japa Vidhi ||

Om Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche |
(Chant 108 times)

Prathamam Shailaputri Cha Dvitiyam Brahmacharini |
Tritiyam Chandraghanteti Kushmandeti Chaturthakam ||

Panchamam Skandamateti Shashtham Katyayani Iti Cha |
Saptamam Kalaratrishcha Mahagauriti Chashtamam |
Navamam Siddhidatri Cha Navadurgah Prakirtitah || 4 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Navarnam Mantrarajanam Navadurga Samanvitam |
Yah Pathech Chraddhaya Nityam Sarvan Kaman Avapnuyat || 5 ||

|| Iti Shri Chandi Path Navarna Vidhi Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ॐ ऐं ह्रीं क्लीं चामुण्डायै विच्चे — यह नवार्ण (नौ अक्षरों का) महामन्त्र है।

इसके ऋषि ब्रह्मा, छन्द गायत्री, देवता चामुण्डा, ऐं बीज, ह्रीं शक्ति, क्लीं कीलक है। चामुण्डा प्रीत्यर्थ जप में विनियोग।

ध्यान: खड्ग, चक्र, गदा, बाण, धनुष, शूल, भुशुण्डी, मस्तक, शंख धारण करती, त्रिनयना, सर्वांगभूषित, नीलमणि कान्ति वाली, दशमुखी-दशपादा महाकालिका — जिनकी स्तुति ब्रह्मा ने विष्णु के सोते समय मधु-कैटभ वध हेतु की।

महालक्ष्मी: अक्षमाला, परशु, गदा, कुलिश, पद्म, धनुष, कुण्डिका, दण्ड, शक्ति, खड्ग, ढाल, कमल, घण्टा, सुरापात्र, शूल, पाश, सुदर्शन धारिणी, प्रसन्नानना।

महासरस्वती: घण्टा, शूल, हल, शंख, मूसल, चक्र, धनुष, बाण धारिणी, गौरी के शरीर से उत्पन्न, त्रिजगत् की आधार।

नवार्ण मन्त्र का १०८ बार जप करें। नवदुर्गा: शैलपुत्री, ब्रह्मचारिणी, चन्द्रघण्टा, कूष्माण्डा, स्कन्दमाता, कात्यायनी, कालरात्रि, महागौरी, सिद्धिदात्री।

नवार्ण मन्त्रराज का नवदुर्गासहित श्रद्धापूर्वक नित्य पाठ करने से सर्वकाम प्राप्त होते हैं।
benefits: Complete Navarna Mantra vidhi — the king of Devi mantras
Includes dhyana for Mahakali, Mahalakshmi and Mahasaraswati
108 repetitions of Navarna mantra fulfills all desires
Essential preparation for Durga Saptashati / Chandi Path recitation
Most powerful Shakti mantra for Navaratri worship
description: Chandi Path Navarna Vidhi (चण्डी पाठ नवार्ण विधि) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Goddess Durga. Rooted in Durga Saptashati / Markandeya Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to complete Navarna Mantra vidhi — the king of Devi mantras, 108 repetitions of Navarna mantra fulfills all desires, and include dhyana for Mahakali, Mahalakshmi and Mahasaraswati. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri, Chaitra Navratri, and Durga Ashtami for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 5 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Chandi Path Navarna Vidhi in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Chandi Path Navarna Vidhi?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chandi Path Navarna Vidhi (चण्डी पाठ नवार्ण विधि) is the prescribed pre-recitation procedure (vidhi) for the Navarna mantra — Om Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche — that must be performed before beginning the Durga Saptashati (Chandi Path) recitation. The Navarna (literally nine-letter / nine-syllable) mantra is the supreme bija mantra of Goddess Chamunda — the wrathful aspect of Devi Durga who slew the demons Chanda and Munda — and is considered the king of all Devi mantras (mantra-rajan). This 5-verse vidhi text contains: (1) the formal Viniyoga declaration (specifying the Rishi as Brahma, Chhanda as Gayatri, Devata as Chamunda, with bija Aim, shakti Hrim and kilakam Klim), (2) the dhyana shlokas of the Tridevi (Mahakali, Mahalakshmi, Mahasaraswati) from chapters 1, 4 and 5 of the Durga Saptashati, (3) the prescription for 108 japa of the Navarna mantra, (4) the enumeration of the Navadurga (nine forms of Durga), and (5) the phala-shruti. (Source: Durga Saptashati / Markandeya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the Navarna Mantra Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navarna mantra Om Aim Hrim Klim Chamundayai Vichche is the supreme Devi-bija mantra — every syllable carries enormous tantric power. Decoded: (1) Om — the Pranava, the cosmic sound-seed of Brahman; (2) Aim — the Saraswati bija, the Vagishvari-shakti of pure knowledge and speech; (3) Hrim — the Maya-bija of Mahalakshmi-Bhuvaneshwari, the Shakti of cosmic enchantment; (4) Klim — the Kama-bija of Krishna-Kali, the magnetic attractive power; (5) Chamundayai — to Chamunda, the form of Kali who slew the demons Chanda and Munda in chapter 7 of the Durga Saptashati; (6) Vichche — the secret kilaka-bija that locks and seals the mantra's power. The mantra thus invokes simultaneously all three Mahavidyas (Saraswati-Aim, Lakshmi-Hrim, Kali-Klim) directed to Chamunda — making it the most condensed and complete Devi mantra. The formal viniyoga states Brahma is the Rishi (since Brahma first received and chanted it during Madhu-Kaitabha vadha in chapter 1 of the Saptashati), Gayatri is the Chhanda, and Chamunda is the Devata. (Source: Devi Atharvashirsha; Durga Saptashati.)

seoFaqs.question: Why must the Navarna Vidhi be performed before the Durga Saptashati?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navarna Vidhi is mandatory before any recitation of the Durga Saptashati for several reasons. (1) Adhikara-prapti — the Navarna mantra grants the qualification (adhikara) to recite the Saptashati; without 108 prior japa of the Navarna, the path itself bears no fruit, as declared in the Saptashati paddhati. (2) Mantra-shakti-prajagarana — the Navarna awakens the dormant shakti within the recited verses of the Saptashati; the entire text is considered a giant elaboration of this single mantra. (3) The dhyana shlokas of the three episodes (Mahakali, Mahalakshmi, Mahasaraswati) included in the Vidhi prepare the mind to visualise the three Devis whose deeds occupy the three charitas (sections) of the Saptashati. (4) Viniyoga-anushthana — declaring the Rishi, Chhanda, Devata, bija, shakti and kilaka establishes proper Vedic protocol. (5) Pratyabhinna-grahana — receiving the formal initiation flow before approaching the supreme Devi text. Many Devi-upasakas chant only the Navarna Vidhi as a complete daily practice when time does not permit the full Saptashati. (Source: Durga Saptashati paddhati; Mantra Mahodadhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the three Tridevi dhyana shlokas included in the Navarna Vidhi?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navarna Vidhi includes the three classical dhyana shlokas of the Mahakali, Mahalakshmi and Mahasaraswati — corresponding to the three charitas of the Durga Saptashati. (1) Mahakali Dhyana (verse 1, dark blue, ten-handed): Khadgam chakra-gada-ishu-chapa-parighan shulam bhushundim shirah, shankham sandadhatim karaih trinayanam sarva-anga-bhusha-vritam, nila-ashma-dyutim asya-pada-dashakam seve Mahakalikam — the dark-sapphire ten-faced ten-footed Mahakali who Brahma praised to slay Madhu-Kaitabha when Vishnu was sleeping. (2) Mahalakshmi Dhyana (verse 2, golden, eighteen-handed, on lotus): Akshasrak-parashu-gada-ishu-kulisham padmam dhanu-kundikam... seve Sairibha-mardinim iha Mahalakshmim sarojasthitam — Mahalakshmi the slayer of buffalo-demon Mahishasura. (3) Mahasaraswati Dhyana (verse 3, moon-white, born from Gauri's body, eight-handed): Ghanta-shula-halani shankha-musale chakram dhanuh sayakam... Mahapurvam atra Sarasvatim anubhaje Shumbhadi-daitya-ardinim — Mahasaraswati the slayer of Shumbha and Nishumbha. These three dhyanas open the three charitas of the Saptashati. (Source: Durga Saptashati Pradhanika Rahasya; Markandeya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Navarna Vidhi and Mantra be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navarna Mantra is most powerfully recited during all nine days of Sharad Navratri (Ashvin Shukla Pratipada to Navami) and Chaitra Navratri (Chaitra Shukla Pratipada to Navami), Durga Ashtami (the eighth day of any bright fortnight), Mahanavami, Vijayadashami, Friday (Shukravar — the day of Lakshmi and Devi), Tuesday (Mangalvar — the day of Mahakali), every Ashtami, Navami and Chaturdashi, and during Solar / Lunar eclipses (sun and lunar grahanas) when mantra-shakti is multiplied many fold. The standard practice is 108 japa daily during regular periods, increasing to 1008 daily during Navratri. The Navarna mantra requires either Devi-diksha (formal initiation from a qualified guru) for full bija japa, or the Saptashati paddhati form (without bija decoding) which any devotee may recite. The phala-shruti promises that recitation at sandhya-times (sunrise, noon, sunset, midnight) is most potent — sunrise for siddhi-prapti, noon for shatru-nashana, sunset for shanti, midnight (nishita) for advanced tantric sadhana. (Sources: Durga Saptashati paddhati; Devi Bhagavata Purana 12.)

seoFaqs.question: Who are the Navadurga (nine Durga forms) listed in this Vidhi?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 4 of the Navarna Vidhi enumerates the nine forms of Durga (Navadurga) worshipped one on each day of Navratri — drawn from the Devi Kavacha and the Devi Mahatmya: (1) Shailaputri (Pratipada — daughter of the Himalaya, riding a bull, holding trishula and lotus), (2) Brahmacharini (Dwitiya — the ascetic form, holding kamandalu and rudraksha-mala), (3) Chandraghanta (Tritiya — the warrior with a moon-bell on her forehead, ten-armed), (4) Kushmanda (Chaturthi — the cosmic egg-creator, eight-armed, riding a lion), (5) Skandamata (Panchami — the mother of Skanda-Karttikeya, holding the infant in her lap), (6) Katyayani (Shashthi — daughter of sage Katyayana, the warrior who slew Mahishasura), (7) Kalaratri (Saptami — the dark night-form, four-armed, riding a donkey), (8) Mahagauri (Ashtami — the supremely fair eight-year-old form, four-armed, riding a bull), (9) Siddhidatri (Navami — the bestower of all siddhis, four-armed on a lotus). Each form is worshipped on the corresponding day of Navratri. The Navarna Vidhi's enumeration unifies the Tridevi dhyana with the Navadurga sequence — making it a comprehensive Devi-upasana paddhati. (Source: Devi Kavacha; Markandeya Purana Devi Mahatmya.)
